Amended MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jul-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing RE: PLAINTIFF CODY CARTER's Amended MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD.

Appearances (via Zoom):

Alister Wong, Esq., Deldar Legal, (844) 335-3271, for Plaintiff.

Not Argued/Contested. Ruling - Discussed. Counsel provides an update regarding the "breakdown in communication" as stated in the motion papers. At the conclusion of discussion, the Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record is granted.

Counsel is to submit a proposed order on the mandatory Judicial Council form.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. (CCP 437c(p)(2).)

Defendant contends that plaintiff's action is time-barred because he failed to file it within six months after defendant sent him its notice of rejection of claim. (See Gov. Code Sec. 945.6(a)(1).)

Defendant's notice of rejection, however, was defective and did not trigger the six-month period. Government Code Sec. 945.6(a)(1) requires that the notice be given in accordance with Government Code Sec. 913. Section 913, subdivision (a) states that written notice of rejection of a claim "or the inaction that is deemed rejection under Section 912.4 shall be given in the manner prescribed by Section 915.4." (Italics added.) Section 915.4, subdivision (a) states notices "shall be given by any of" three specified methods (personal delivery, mail, or email). In this case, defendant attempted to provide notice by mail. The statute allows "[m]ailing the notice to the address, if any, stated in the claim ... as the address to which the person presenting the claim ... desires notices to be sent or, if no such address is stated in the claim ..., by mailing the notice to the address, if any, of the claimant as stated in the claim ...." (Sec. 915.4, subd. (a)(2).)

In this case, defendant failed to send the notice of rejection to the address listed in the claim-190 Mill Street, San Rafael, CA 94901. (Defendant's Table of Evidence, Ex. D.) Rather, defendant allegedly sent the notice to plaintiff's counsel. Defendant likely took this course of conduct because on August 18, 2020, plaintiff's counsel sent a letter to defendant stating: "Please direct all communication regarding this matter to this law firm." (Id.)

Notwithstanding this letter, the service was still defective. In Cavey v. Tualla (2021) 69 Cal.App.5th 310, 345-346, the court explains that if defendant is to send notice to an alternative address, that address should be listed in the claim itself. "The claim submitted by Lark Chiropractic did not state an address to which desired notices should be sent. As a result, the mailing address required by statute was 'the address ... of the claimant as stated in the claim.' (Sec. 915.4, subd. (a)(2).)" (Cavey, 69 Cal.App.5th at 345.) Cavey notes that the statute is unambiguous and defendant cannot re-write it even if plaintiff has counsel. (Id. at 346.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
